def solve(landStartTime, landDuration, waterStartTime, waterDuration)
  n = landStartTime.length
  m = waterStartTime.length

  # Calculate all possible end times for land and water attractions
  land_end_times = []
  water_end_times = []

  # For each land attraction, calculate the end time if it's taken first
  land_end_times = landStartTime.each_with_index.map do |start, i|
    start + landDuration[i]
  end

  # For each water attraction, calculate the end time if it's taken first
  water_end_times = waterStartTime.each_with_index.map do |start, j|
    start + waterDuration[j]
  end

  # Check all possible combinations of one land and one water attraction
  min_time = Float::INFINITY

  # Try all land attractions first, then water
  land_end_times.each_with_index do |land_end, i|
    # Find the earliest possible start time for water after land ends
    water_start = [land_end, waterStartTime.min].max
    water_end = water_start + waterDuration.min
    min_time = [min_time, water_end].min
  end

  # Try all water attractions first, then land
  water_end_times.each_with_index do |water_end, j|
    # Find the earliest possible start time for land after water ends
    land_start = [water_end, landStartTime.min].max
    land_end = land_start + landDuration.min
    min_time = [min_time, land_end].min
  end

  min_time
end
